notDoneOrPartial.ask: Bug: pasting the BlitzOS agent link into a chat — the agent fetches agents.md but says the BlitzOS tools aren't in its toolset, so it summarizes instead of acting
notDoneOrPartial.evidence:
This is a real, reproducible consequence of the current design — there is NO code, doc, or UI change addressing it, and it is not even filed as an open issue (issues/open/ contains only server-mode-deployment.md).

Root cause (architecture is intentional): BlitzOS uses the agent-socket "no MCP" model. Tools are NOT registered into any agent's toolset — they are plain HTTP POST endpoints (`POST $BASE/<tool>`). The relay serves the operating doc at the pasted URL.
- /Users/palash/Projects/teeny/packages/BlitzOS/src/main/agentSocket.ts:57-411 — connect() registers tools only as relay HTTP endpoints; passes BlitzOS's own agentsMd.
- /Users/palash/Projects/teeny/packages/BlitzOS/src/main/blitzos-agents.md:14-18 — "You reach BlitzOS over plain HTTPS, no MCP, no SDK ... Call tools at POST $BASE/<tool>" and "FIRST: GET $BASE/tools.json".
- vendor SDK /Users/palash/Projects/teeny/packages/BlitzOS/vendor/agent-socket-sdk/dist/agents-md.js:30-37 — the "## How to call tools" preamble = "<method> $BASE<path> with a JSON body". So even the framework doc just tells the agent to issue HTTP calls itself.

For ANY of this to work, the agent must have a tool that can issue an HTTP POST (Bash/curl, code execution, or a POST-capable fetch). The repo's own notes say exactly this:
- /Users/palash/Projects/teeny/packages/BlitzOS/plans/working-stream.md:203 — real-agent test is `claude -p "<paste-URL>..." --dangerously-skip-permissions` with the explicit note "claude has Bash/curl -> it POSTs the tools".
- /Users/palash/Projects/teeny/packages/BlitzOS/preview/RUNNING.md:51 — "paste into a code/HTTP-capable agent (or `claude -p`)".

A plain web chat (Claude.ai / ChatGPT web UI) without a POST-capable tool fetches agents.md, reads that the "tools" are HTTP endpoints it must POST to, finds nothing in its toolset that can do that, and so it summarizes the doc — exactly the reported symptom ("those tools aren't available to me in this chat").

The user-facing instructions actively cause the bug by omitting the capability requirement:
- /Users/palash/Projects/teeny/packages/BlitzOS/src/renderer/src/App.tsx:669 (the in-app "Connect AI" panel) — "Paste this URL into Claude / ChatGPT and ask it to open windows, post-its, etc." (no caveat).
- /Users/palash/Projects/teeny/packages/BlitzOS/README.md:24 — "paste the URL into Claude / ChatGPT" (no caveat).

No detection of a no-tool environment, no fallback, and no corrected guidance exists anywhere in committed code.
notDoneOrPartial.userFacingGap: End-to-end, pasting the link into a generic web chat (Claude.ai / ChatGPT web UI, which is exactly what the README and the in-app "Connect AI" panel tell the user to do) will NOT work: that environment cannot issue the HTTP POST calls the tools require, so the agent reads agents.md and summarizes instead of acting. It only works in an agent that has a POST-capable tool (Claude Code, `claude -p` with Bash/curl, or a code-execution chat). The fix would be either (a) correct the user-facing copy in App.tsx:669 and README:24 to specify a code/HTTP-capable agent and ideally suggest `claude -p`, and/or (b) have agents.md instruct the agent to use its own fetch/code tool and gracefully say "open me in a tool-capable agent" when it can't — none of which is implemented.

notDoneOrPartial.ask: Bug: canvas keeps jumping back to HackerNews — typing on Google sent me back to HN ("i keep going back to hackernews for some reason, i was just typing on google and it took me back to hn, is that some bug?")
notDoneOrPartial.evidence:
This is a REAL, confirmed bug in the committed code — not yet fixed. Root cause is a two-part gap I traced end-to-end:

1. In-webview navigation is never synced back to the store/disk. The HN window is a `web` surface created at `src/renderer/src/App.tsx:567` (`createSurface({ kind:'web', url:'https://news.ycombinator.com', title:'Hacker News' })`). It renders as `<webview src={surface.url}>` at `src/renderer/src/components/SurfaceFrame.tsx:402`. When the user navigates inside that webview (HN -> Google), `surface.url` in the zustand store still says `https://news.ycombinator.com`. I grepped the entire renderer: the ONLY webview listeners are `dom-ready` (zoom + webContents registration, SurfaceFrame.tsx:78/98). There is NO `did-navigate` / `did-navigate-in-page` listener writing the user's navigation back into the store. (A prior fix, commit 61b9d8a, added URL sync for SERVER-mode address-bar nav only — the Electron `<webview>` desktop path the user is on has no equivalent.) The on-disk `.weblink` file also still holds the original HN url (`src/main/workspace.mjs:110`).

2. Reconcile re-applies the stale HN url onto the live surface, forcing a reload. The folder watcher (`src/main/workspace-host.mjs` `scheduleReconcile` / `doReconcile`, fired by `fs.watch` on the workspace dir + `.blitzos/` on any EXTERNAL edit — e.g. the agent writing a file, the normal mode of an "Agent OS") rescans disk via `reconcileWorkspace`, reads the `.weblink` back as `url: 'https://news.ycombinator.com'` (`src/main/workspace.mjs:453` via `safeUrl`), and broadcasts a `reconcile` action. The renderer applies it in `applyReconcile` (`src/renderer/src/store.ts:424-455`): for a non-runtime surface that is NOT `editingId`, it overwrites `url: w.url` (store.ts:446) with the disk/HN value. Critically `editingId` is set ONLY by NoteWidget's textarea focus (`src/renderer/src/components/NoteWidget.tsx:16`), never by a web surface — so the `if (w.id === s.editingId) return live` guard at store.ts:437 does NOT protect a web window the user is browsing. React then re-renders `<webview src=...>` with the HN url, and Electron's reactive webview `src` reloads the guest back to HN — kicking the user off Google. This is deterministic for any reconcile that lands while the user is on a different page than the stored url.

The #47 fix (commit bbd4515 "Reconcile no longer clobbers the surface you're actively editing") only covers note text via `editingId`; it does not cover in-webview navigation, so the bug survives.
notDoneOrPartial.userFacingGap: The user's webview windows silently snap back to their original URL whenever a workspace reconcile fires (most often when the connected agent writes anything to the workspace folder, but also any external file edit). So browsing inside an opened web window — typing on Google, reading any page they navigated to — gets yanked back to the surface's starting URL (Hacker News) with no warning, losing their place and any typed input. It is a genuine bug, and it is not fixed in the current tree (no `did-navigate` sync, and `applyReconcile` overwrites `url` for any non-note surface).

notDoneOrPartial.ask: Fix Discord OAuth which fails with 'invalid oauth2 redirect uri'
notDoneOrPartial.evidence:
No fix exists in committed code; the OAuth path is unchanged from when integrations were first added.

Root cause (confirmed in code):
- /Users/palash/Projects/teeny/packages/BlitzOS/src/main/oauth.ts:9 hard-codes a SINGLE redirect for ALL providers: `REDIRECT_URI = http://127.0.0.1:${REDIRECT_PORT}/callback` (port 8723). It is injected unconditionally into the authorize URL at oauth.ts:126 (`u.searchParams.set('redirect_uri', REDIRECT_URI)`).
- /Users/palash/Projects/teeny/packages/BlitzOS/src/main/integrations.ts:244-256 — the Discord flow opens `https://discord.com/oauth2/authorize` and passes the same `redirect_uri: REDIRECT_URI` (line 255). There is NO Discord-specific override anywhere (grep of src/ shows only the one constant used by all 5 providers).

Why it fails: Discord's authorize endpoint rejects `http://` loopback on a raw IP. Discord only honors the literal hostname `http://localhost...` for an http loopback redirect (or requires https otherwise); `http://127.0.0.1:...` is not accepted, producing the exact "invalid oauth2 redirect uri" page in the system browser. Notably this is the opposite of the repo's own convention in CLAUDE.md ("OAuth redirect is fixed... Use 127.0.0.1, not localhost") — the convention that makes Google/GitHub work is precisely what breaks Discord.

Docs reinforce the broken value: README.md:28 and integrations.config.example.json:28 both tell the user to register `http://127.0.0.1:8723/callback` for Discord, so a user following the instructions still hits the error.

Git evidence: `git log -- src/main/oauth.ts src/main/integrations.ts` shows only the original `52830bc` (widget system) and `2bae799` (init) commits — no Discord/redirect fix. `git status` / `git diff HEAD` on both files is clean (no uncommitted fix). Recent commits (window system, files/folders, workspaces) touch nothing in the OAuth path.

The error appears on Discord's page (during shell.openExternal of the authorize URL) before any callback, so it never reaches the app's own error handling in ConnectPanel.tsx — matching the user's symptom exactly.
notDoneOrPartial.userFacingGap: Discord remains unconnectable. Clicking "Connect" on Discord opens the browser to Discord's authorize page, which shows "Invalid OAuth2 redirect_uri" instead of a consent screen. The user can never complete the Discord SSO flow. Even registering the exact URL the README/example config instruct (`http://127.0.0.1:8723/callback`) does not help, because Discord rejects http+raw-IP loopback. A fix would need a Discord-specific redirect (e.g. `http://localhost:8723/callback`, with the loopback server still bound to 127.0.0.1 and the URL/text updated for Discord), plus updating README.md and integrations.config.example.json to register the localhost form for Discord.

DOM/event flow (the crux): React mounts at `#root` (src/renderer/index.html), and the ONLY canvas wheel listener is a native `el.addEventListener('wheel', onWheel, {passive:false})` on `#root-canvas` (src/renderer/src/App.tsx:133, where rootRef = the `#root-canvas` div at App.tsx:600). `#root-canvas` is a DESCENDANT of React's `#root`. Two scrollable native panels carry `onWheel={(e) => e.stopPropagation()}` — ChatPanel.tsx:40 and ActivityPanel.tsx:57 (added in commit ccbf471). React 18.3.1 (package.json) delegates synthetic events at the `#root` container, so a React `stopPropagation()` on a panel CANNOT stop the native bubble-phase listener sitting on the intermediate `#root-canvas` node — the wheel event still reaches `onWheel` and runs panBy/zoomAt.

Mitigation that masks the bug in the common case: the native handler early-returns in the default `desktop` mode — App.tsx:127 `if (useDesktop.getState().mode !== 'canvas') return` (no preventDefault, no panBy). So in desktop mode the canvas never pans, and web/app `<webview>`/`<iframe>` surfaces (no onWheel stopPropagation anywhere — confirmed grep) scroll normally only because pan is globally disabled there, not because propagation is stopped. In `canvas` (double-Cmd control) mode the handler runs panBy/zoomAt, and the panels' stopPropagation does NOT block it → scrolling a panel still pans the canvas underneath.

Commit-message vs reality: commit 4b92942 ("Fix merged preview: shim parity + scroll propagation") claims "the canvas wheel/down handlers now stopPropagation so input drives the page, not the canvas," but its diff touches ONLY preview/agentos-shim.js (git show 4b92942 --stat: 1 file, +5/-2) — the App.tsx canvas handlers were NOT changed by it. The distinct #26 "overscroll" item (commit 189dd65) is a different bug (Mac two-finger swipe-nav, fixed via `overscroll-behavior:none`), not this one. Working tree is clean (git status empty).
notDoneOrPartial.userFacingGap: In the default desktop mode the user won't see canvas panning while scrolling (pan is disabled there), so the most-common scenario is OK. But the requested fix ("stop propagation") is not correctly implemented: in canvas/control mode, scrolling a chat/activity panel still pans the canvas as well as scrolling the panel — exactly the "scrolls both" behavior reported. The stopPropagation handlers that were added are ineffective because the canvas wheel listener is a native addEventListener on an ancestor DOM node, which React synthetic stopPropagation can't reach. A correct fix would gate the App.tsx native handler on event.target (skip when over a panel), use a native non-passive wheel+stopPropagation listener on the panel element, or check a "scrollable" hit-test before panBy.

Code trace (all present, both Electron + browser-preview paths):
- /Users/palash/Projects/teeny/packages/BlitzOS/src/renderer/src/components/ChatPanel.tsx:24-33 — send() appends the user bubble locally and calls window.agentOS.sendMessage(text). No throw, no swallowed error.

- Backend /Users/palash/Projects/teeny/packages/BlitzOS/preview/backend.mjs:1043-1053 — route '/api/os/user-message' calls emitUserMessage(text). Path/prefix match confirmed.
- /Users/palash/Projects/teeny/packages/BlitzOS/src/main/perception-core.mjs:186-204 emitUserMessage pushes a moment {trigger:'message', message} into the /events moment log and wakes waiters.
- Reply path also wired: backend.mjs:673-682 '/say' tool -> broadcast({type:'chat',text}); App.tsx:300-310 onAction 'chat' appends an {role:'agent'} message into the chat surface (creates one if none). Electron path mirrors this via src/preload/index.ts:113-116 -> ipc 'os:user-message' (src/main/osActions.ts:113).

Live runtime proof (renderer up on :5174, proxies /api to backend):
- POST http://127.0.0.1:5174/api/os/user-message {"text":"ping"} -> HTTP 200 (endpoint accepts and returns ok).
- GET /api/os/agent-url -> a real agent paste URL is minted (agentsocket.dev/...).

Root cause / gap: BlitzOS has NO built-in agent. A chat message only produces a visible reply if an external AI has pasted the agent-socket URL and is actively polling /events and calling /say. So with no connected/idle agent, the message is correctly stored but nothing ever replies = "nothing happened" from the user's view. The empty-state hint (ChatPanel.tsx:43-46, "Connect an AI first") is the only signal, and it disappears once any message exists.
notDoneOrPartial.userFacingGap: If the user sends a chat message without a live AI connected (the default state — there is no built-in agent), they get a perpetual "working…" bubble and no reply, with no error or "no agent connected" feedback. The mechanism works, but the experience is indistinguishable from a broken feature. The UX does not gate sending on connection state, does not surface "no agent is listening," and the "working…" indicator never times out.

Renderer reality:
- Canvas wheel handler is a NATIVE listener on #root-canvas (App.tsx:136-145), guarded by `if (mode !== 'canvas') return` (line 138). Default mode is 'desktop' (store.ts:179).
- In default desktop mode there is genuinely NO double-scroll: the handler early-returns, pan/zoom is intentionally off, so a window scrolls only itself.
- In canvas/control mode (reached by double-tap Cmd), the native handler runs panBy/zoomAt. Native panels (ChatPanel.tsx:40, ActivityPanel.tsx:57) try to block it with React `onWheel={(e)=>e.stopPropagation()}`, but this is ARCHITECTURALLY INEFFECTIVE: React 18 delegates events at #root (index.html), which is the PARENT of #root-canvas where the native listener lives — so the native listener fires during native bubbling before React's synthetic dispatch, and synthetic stopPropagation cannot cancel it.
- web (<webview>) and app/srcdoc (<iframe>) surfaces don't bubble wheel across the document boundary, so those don't reach the canvas handler. The exposure is native scrollable panels in canvas mode.
- No onWheel exists on the SurfaceFrame window div; no target-based exclusion (e.g. e.target.closest('.window')) was ever added to App.tsx (verified via git log -S).
notDoneOrPartial.userFacingGap: In control/canvas mode, scrolling inside a native panel (Chat, Activity, etc.) that has its own scrollable content still pans/zooms the canvas underneath — the existing per-panel stopPropagation does not prevent it because the canvas wheel listener is a native listener on an ancestor of React's #root delegation point. The intended fix (canvas wheel handler ignoring events over a window, or attaching the listener so panels can cancel it) was never applied to the Electron renderer — only to the browser preview shim. In the default desktop mode the user would not see the bug since canvas pan/zoom is disabled there.

The widget SYSTEM is real and proven (commit 52830bc "Widget system: agent-browsable, integration-backed srcdoc widgets + consent bridge"): agents browse/fork/author/spawn sandboxed srcdoc HTML widgets backed by connected OAuth integrations over a consent-gated window.blitz bridge. Verified e2e in headless Chromium + real relay (plans/working-stream.md L195-204; CLAUDE.md). BUT the data a widget can read is a CLOSED allowlist — PROVIDER_DATA in src/main/widget-catalog.mjs (L134-163) — containing EXACTLY two resources: discord/guilds (servers) and github/repos. There is NO "unread messages" resource: no gmail/messages, no discord DMs/messages, no slack. Any other (provider,resource) pair is rejected 404 by design (SSRF guard) — confirmed in preview/backend.mjs L815-836 and the catalog's hasOwn check. Gmail is a connectable integration with gmail.readonly scope (backend.mjs L77-80) but is deliberately absent from PROVIDER_DATA, so widgets cannot fetch mail. The team's own working-stream.md L206 lists this as NOT done: "more provider resources (gmail messages, jira issues)". The only authored-widget artifact on disk (widgets/authored/discord-list.html — gitignored/untracked, NOT committed) lists Discord SERVERS as a numbered list, not unread messages.</parameter>
<parameter name="userFacingGap">If the user asks the agent to "make a widget to show all my unread messages," the agent can create the widget shell, but it has no data source for unread messages. The bridge only serves discord/guilds (your servers) and github/repos — neither is messages, and nothing exposes "unread." A real attempt would either 404 on the data fetch or degrade to showing servers/repos (as the existing discord-list.html does). The user would NOT get a working unread-messages widget. To make this real, a gmail/messages (or discord DMs / slack) resource must be added to the PROVIDER_DATA registry first — explicitly tracked as future work, not built.

WHAT IS NOT PROVEN / NOT DONE (the actual vision):

- Auto-open the user's real accounts (Discord/Gmail) as tabs around a workspace: agent-os-dynamic-architecture.md Pillar 2 = NOT DONE; nothing in preview/backend.mjs auto-opens on launch; no on-disk evidence the brain ever opened Discord/Gmail.
- AI reading open page content -> surfacing SUGGESTED REPLIES in a widget the user can Send from: the widget->OS write path is still unwired. SurfaceFrame.tsx:189 rejects every bridge op except 'data' ("unsupported op"); blitz.tool() exists client-side (widget-bridge.ts:65) but has no server branch. grep for send_reply/sendReply = ZERO matches anywhere. grep for `transient` descriptor flag = ZERO. Pillar 6 in the arch doc = "NOT DONE (mechanism partial)".
- Attention/follow-mode (agent moves the human's view): store.focusAndZoom math exists but NO os:action maps to it. App.tsx handles only hydrate/switch/reconcile/create/move/update/close/goToPrimary/chat/activity/group — no focus/follow/set_mode case. Pillar 8 = "NOT DONE (math exists, unreachable)".
- The honest self-assessment in plans/agent-os-dynamic-architecture.md scores the 10 vision pillars: only Pillars 3 and 5 are PARTIAL, Pillar 10 PARTIAL; Pillars 1,2,4,6,7,8,9 = NOT DONE. Substrate ~70%; the autonomous-brain + governance + attention + profile/memory layers are not built.

Key files: src/main/agent-runner.mjs, src/main/blitzos-agents.md (On connect assembly + named CS scenario), src/renderer/src/components/SurfaceFrame.tsx:189 (op:'data'-only gate), src/renderer/src/App.tsx:257-338 (wired os:action cases, no follow/focus), plans/agent-os-dynamic-architecture.md (the honest pillar-by-pillar status table, §2), plans/working-stream.md (#48 claim), preview/.workspace/Home (the example.com/"cs" artifacts that undercut the assembled-desktop proof).
notDoneOrPartial.userFacingGap: A user expecting the JARVIS demo from the quote would be disappointed end-to-end. The dynamic-OS exists only as: paste a URL into an AI chat (or run a `claude -p` brain), and IF a brain is running it CAN open windows and arrange a desktop via the tools. But: (1) it does not boot into a tailored desktop by itself — a brain must be spawned (opt-in env BLITZ_AGENT) and is slow/flaky (self-restarts every 5-8 min); (2) the specific demo of a support persona auto-opening Discord+Gmail tabs has never actually been produced — the only saved workspace is full of 21 dummy example.com links and 2-byte "cs" notes; (3) the headline payoff — AI reads the open page and shows a suggested-reply widget you can press Send on — is not implemented at all (widget can't write back to the OS, no send_reply, no follow-mode to bring it to your attention). The polished, proven part is the window manager / canvas mechanics, not the autonomous JARVIS behavior. The GUI feel itself is also still unverified on a real Mac display (no display in this sandbox; only typecheck/build/headless-store tests run).
